CZ commented, "One must be able to rise and fall... maintaining a balanced mindset when demoted is true skill." His long-termism focuses on the North Star (creating value), but the path and methods are constantly optimized based on feedback. He doesn't stubbornly resist, he's flexible. This is the most counterintuitive and most painful point about the working class's mindset in the entire book. CZ rarely advocates "hard work" in the book. He even says, "I've become intolerant of people and things that waste time." He cut out lengthy meetings, meaningless small talk, television, news, sports, and music, maximizing communication efficiency. His true leap in wealth stemmed from several key choices: 1. Choosing the right path: In 2013, he chose to leave the comfort of his "retirement home" company and go all-in on blockchain. 2. Choosing the right model: In 2017, he chose to operate a "crypto-to-crypto exchange," circumventing fiat currency regulations and rapidly globalizing. 3. Choosing Values ​​In the "94 ban" and numerous hacking incidents, choosing to "protect users," even at the cost of significant short-term losses, earns long-term trust. He wrote: "When facing users, always choose to do the right thing, not the easy thing. Protect your users, and success and wealth will come unexpectedly." Effort can only make you move faster on a predetermined track; choice, however, directly helps you switch to a faster track, or even build a car. Ordinary people struggle daily with how to row harder, while experts look up at the wind and choose which ship to board. The Price of Becoming the Richest Man: The Deification and the Devouring of Ordinary People. Reading this book, if you only see wealth and success, you're being too superficial. What moved me most was CZ's vulnerability and perseverance as a "human being." The Alienated "God" and "Demon". After Binance's success, CZ was elevated to a god-like status. "The events he attended were always packed with people, with long lines for photos. Some people photoshopped his face onto Jesus' face and hung it in their cars for good luck; some people flew from afar just to shake his hand." During his lowest point, he was trampled into the mud again. "Regulators are here, lawsuits are here, and the headlines have changed their tone. The same person doing the same thing, yesterday it was legendary, today it's a crime. Those who once called him a savior now call him a cancer in the crypto world." (Excerpt from "Binance Life") He Yi succinctly puts it in the preface: "The masses are enthusiastic about creating gods, and also enthusiastic about destroying gods. A god is created, a god is smashed, broken into mud, trampled on a few times, and then replaced by another. But he is always just himself." We have never truly seen him; we have only seen our own inner desires, fantasies, and fears through him. Those who crave sudden wealth regard him as a god of wealth; those who abhor risk see him as a gambler; those who need idols mold him into Jesus; those who seek sinners accuse him of being a demon.

（From the illustration of "Binance Life")

The Paradox of Wealth and Freedom

In 2023, he decided to voluntarily fly to the United States to plead guilty.

He told his team, "If I don't go, the Biden administration will kill Binance, BNB holders will suffer severely, and the industry might regress to what it was 10 years ago." He traded his personal freedom for the survival of the company and the industry. In an American prison, he lost all his external labels: billionaire, Binance CEO, industry leader. Only after he was stripped of everything did he realize what was truly important. "I don't miss my job... I don't miss parties or any luxuries." "Sometimes I do miss good food and my own bed. But that's okay." "What I really miss are people. I miss my family and friends. I realize that the people around me are the most important." His father told him on his deathbed, "I've lived a good life. I came from my hometown and saw the world. I raised two excellent children and lived the way I wanted. I'm content and happy with this journey." This father and son, at the pinnacle of wealth and the end of their lives, reached the same consensus: the value of life lies in experiences and relationships, not numbers and labels. CZ's "Giggle Academy," a charitable project launched after his release from prison, aims to provide free education to hundreds of millions of children worldwide. What drives him is not fame or fortune, but those words: "I am a beneficiary of education…they learn so they can find jobs, and if some of them are willing to give back to society after benefiting, this cause can continue." The man who has been through the rain himself is finally beginning to hold an umbrella for many more. What can ordinary people "steal" from CZ?

Leaving aside the allure of cryptocurrency, CZ's growth trajectory and mindset are highly replicable for ordinary people in their careers, entrepreneurship, and investments.

（From the illustration of "Binance Life")

3 reusable core principles

Focus: Do one thing to the extreme

Before founding Binance, CZ had already provided system services to more than 30 exchanges.

data.title: Huobi Founder-Linked Avenir Team Joins Bitfire As Firm Builds Regulated Bitcoin Derivatives And ETF Based Strategy
data.description:
Li Lin, the crypto entrepreneur behind the rise of Huobi (now HTX), is pulling his trading expertise back into the spotlight through Hong Kong-listed Bitfire Group.

The move brings his family office’s trading team and systems directly into a public company structure, as Hong Kong continues to position itself as a regulated hub for digital assets.

Li Lin Repositions Trading Engine Into Bitfire’s Hong Kong Push

Li Lin, the crypto entrepreneur behind the rise of Huobi (now HTX), is pulling his trading expertise back into the spotlight through Hong Kong-listed Bitfire Group.

The move brings his family office’s trading team and systems directly into a public company structure, as Hong Kong continues to position itself as a regulated hub for digital assets.

Bloomberg: Li Lin Transfers 20-Person Trading Team to Hong Kong Stock Exchange-Listed Company Bitfire

Li Lin is transferring the entire 20-person trading team from his family office, Avenir Group, to Bitfire Group, a Hong Kong-listed wealth management company.

— Telbloggram (@Telbloggram) April 22, 2026

Bitfire Group confirmed on Wednesday, 22 April 2026, that it has agreed to acquire the investment team and trading systems of Avenir Group, Li’s family office, for US$1.6 million.

Li, already the largest shareholder in Bitfire, is effectively embedding his private trading setup into the listed firm’s operations.

Li Lin, founder of Huobi (now HTX) and a long-time crypto entrepreneur, is expanding his trading operations in Hong Kong through Bitfire Group.

The shift follows his exit from a controlling stake in Huobi in 2022, which was sold for about US$1 billion to Justin Sun.

Since then, Li has been building out Avenir, which has grown into a major institutional investor in Bitcoin exchange-traded products.

What Is Bitfire Buying From Avenir Group

The deal is centred on Avenir’s trading infrastructure and investment team, which will now be absorbed into Bitfire’s wealth management business.

Bitfire CEO Livio Weng said the company is positioning itself to scale regulated crypto investment services using this setup.

Livio Weng is the chief executive of Bitfire Group, leading its expansion into regulated Bitcoin investment products and institutional crypto wealth management in Hong Kong.

Pointing to the rising interest from firms already holding Bitcoin but lacking structured ways to generate returns, he said,

“Market demand for such products is huge.”

Alpha BTC Targets $760 Million Bitcoin Allocation

At the centre of Bitfire’s expansion is a new regulated strategy called Alpha BTC, designed to generate returns through Bitcoin-linked derivatives, including options and instruments tied to Bitcoin ETFs such as BlackRock’s IBIT.

Weng said the firm aims to attract investment equivalent to more than 10,000 Bitcoins within a year, worth roughly US$760 million at current prices.

The product is designed for both crypto-native investors and Hong Kong-listed companies with Bitcoin exposure.

Bitfire Group targets institutional investors with new bitcoin strategy

Bitfire Group is expanding in Hong Kong by acquiring Li Lin’s trading team to offer the Alpha BTC strategy. This service uses derivatives to generate gains for companies that are currently just holding their… pic.twitter.com/SpE367Bcl9

— BABA CRYPTO (@babacryptoio) April 22, 2026

Why Are Hong Kong Companies the Focus

Bitfire estimates that at least 40 Hong Kong-listed firms already hold Bitcoin, creating a potential base for structured yield products built around digital assets.

Avenir itself has been a major institutional player in Bitcoin ETFs, holding 18.3 million shares of BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust, valued at around US$908 million at the end of 2025.

The strategy reflects a shift from simple holding to active yield generation, as companies look to manage crypto assets within a regulated framework.

Li Lin Family Office Avenir Group Boosts Bitcoin ETF Holdings to 18.3 Million IBIT Shares Worth $1.189B, Fifth Straight Quarter as Asia's Largest Institutional Holder https://t.co/8ZEtHpQHOT

— EN.COINOTAG.COM (@encoinotagcom) November 15, 2025

Hong Kong’s Crypto Strategy Versus Global Policy Gaps

Hong Kong regulators are continuing to push forward with digital asset licensing and oversight, including the recent approval of its first stablecoin issuer licences and new rules for crypto intermediaries.

Eric Yip Chee-hang of the Securities and Futures Commission said the city is now looking beyond its local market.

“We can be a little bit more aspirational now that we have a strong hold locally. We should also expand our influence by increasing exposure internationally.”

Lawmakers are also watching developments in the United States, where crypto legislation has faced delays.

Legislative Council member Duncan Chiu Tat-kun said uncertainty around US bills such as the Genius Act and Clarity Act could stretch into 2027, slowing global regulatory clarity.

“I think they’ve written a very good bill, but the political situation will not give clarity for some time.”

Against that backdrop, Bitfire’s move signals a more structured attempt to build regulated Bitcoin investment products in Hong Kong, anchored by Li Lin’s trading expertise and existing institutional crypto flows.
